title: pediomelum digitatum
intro: Pediomelum digitatum, also known as Lomatium, is a perennial plant in the carrot family native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to combat respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and tuberculosis, due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties. The plant is rich in sesquiterpenes, which are believed to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Herbal preparations of Pediomelum digitatum can be found in teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds.
